claim: SpaceX will be transporting a potentially deadly pathogen to the International Space Station as part of their next launch.

result.claim: SpaceX will be transporting a potentially deadly pathogen to the International Space Station as part of their next launch.
result.reasoning: There is no direct evidence in the provided search results to support or refute the claim that SpaceX will be transporting a potentially deadly pathogen to the International Space Station as part of their next launch. The searches for "SpaceX transporting deadly pathogen to International Space Station," "pathogen transport International Space Station," and "SpaceX biological experiments International Space Station" did not yield any relevant information. The propositions found were either too general (e.g., "Exposure to space conditions had a strong detrimental effect on the survival of the strains" - prop_274250, W2755536802), about different space missions or telescopes (e.g., "NASA’s Habitable Worlds Observatory is a space-based mission" - prop_261711, 40982683), or about general pathogen transmission not related to space transport (e.g., "Pathogenic microorganisms are transmitted by aerosol" - prop_59370, 40356821). No papers specifically mention SpaceX transporting any pathogens, deadly or otherwise, to the ISS.
